summary: Task spec explicitly waives unit tests for this layer: 'TUI callbacks require a real terminal' (TDD Anchor). Acceptance criteria are verified via make build (compilation), make lint (no warnings), and manual smoke test of hotkey, local-mode guard, IO-EACCES warning, idle-conns toggle on procpidstat, age dialog on procpidstat, and cancel/terminate dialogs still blocked on procpidstat. Existing tests in package top must continue to pass — confirmed via go test -race ./top/... (no regression). No tests added, none removed.

metrics.testCoverageAssessment: n/a — TUI presentation layer, no testable pure logic introduced

checks.pyramidPlacement: n/a — no new pure functions; switchViewToProcPidStat is a closure over *app that drives gocui side-effects and a channel send, which cannot be unit-tested without a real gocui.Gui
checks.edgeCases: covered by manual smoke test per acceptance criteria — local-mode-only (covered by Local==false branch), IO-EACCES (covered by ioErr != nil branch), happy path (both branches false)
checks.tddSequence: n/a — task explicitly waives TDD anchor for TUI callbacks
checks.regressionRisk: low — existing top package tests cover dialog.go and config_view.go non-UI paths; race-enabled go test -race ./top/... passes with the diff applied (9.163s, ok). The dialog.go restructure preserves the exact existing message strings, so any test asserting on those would still pass.
checks.compilationVerification: make build succeeds — confirms the new symbol (switchViewToProcPidStat), the new stat package import, the constant references (stat.CollectProcPidStat, stat.CheckIOAvailable) all resolve
checks.lintVerification: make lint (golangci-lint + gosec) reports 0 warnings — confirms no errcheck violation on stat.CheckIOAvailable, no unused-variable warning, no revive complaint on the new function
